title: H. S'man

{{Quote|It's not exactly common for you pilots to come asking for assignments, Farlander. Don't we work you hard enough?|S'man to Keyan Farlander|<ref name="XWOSG" />}}
'''H. S'man''' was a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] who served as one of the chief mission planning [[Officer/Legends|officers]] on the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] during the early stages of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was involved in planning many successful missions for the Alliance during this [[Time/Legends|time]], including the destruction of the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']], ending the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] offensive known as [[Operation Strike Fear]], and the search for the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. His role often saw him working closely with members of [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]], including [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]].

==Biography==
[[File:Independence.png|thumb|250px|S'man served aboard the Mon Calamari Star Cruiser ''Independence''.|left]]
===Early career===
H. S'man joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] early in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="XWOSG" /> At nearly 2.1 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height and close to 135 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]],<ref name="size" /> S'man was considered large for his [[Species/Legends|species]]. Rumors which later circulated among Alliance personnel claimed that S'man's size had precluded him from becoming a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and that he had studied tactics and taken a role as a mission planner to remain involved with starfighter operations. Whatever his reason for becoming a mission planner,<ref name="XWOSG" /> by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]<ref name="1BBY">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY.</ref> S'man held the rank of [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and was one of the chief planning [[Officer/Legends|officers]] aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']].<ref name="XWOSG" />

S'man worked closely with members of [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] in strategic planning, becoming particularly close to [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], and he was frequently involved in briefing the ''Independence''{{'s}} pilots prior to missions. He was credited with planning many of the Alliance's early successes.<ref name="XWOSG" />

===Operation Strike Fear===
{{Quote|All we can know for sure is that the Empire will respond with a warhead to scratch an itch.|S'man|<ref name="XWOSG" />}}
[[File:KeyanFarlander.png|left|thumb|S'man worked regularly with starfighter pilot Keyan Farlander (pictured).]]
Shortly after the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]], the Alliance received intelligence indicating that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was planning a [[Operation Strike Fear|major offensive]]. In response, General Dodonna and his staff aimed to strengthen their own position and uncover the Empire's plans. S'man briefed the pilots aboard the ''Independence'', informing them of the upcoming campaign's goals and the mission plan for its first operation, the [[Battle in the Dellalt system|recovery]] of the defecting [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Bixby]]''.<ref name="XWOSG" />

[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Nogdra]] of the ''Bixby'' revealed that the Empire was using the [[Dellalt system/Legends|Dellalt system]] as a staging point for Operation Strike Fear. S'man selected [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] for a [[Mission in the Dellalt system|reconnaissance mission]] to the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. Before long, Alliance High Command decided the time had come to pursue General Dodonna's stated goal of seeking new allies. To this end, they planned to assist the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] in their [[Sullustan Resistance|fight]] against the Empire, in the hopes of bringing them into the Alliance.<ref name="XWOSG" />

The Rebels had learned that Sullustan prisoners were being transferred from [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and planned a [[Rescue of Sullustan Tech Staff|raid]] to rescue them. In order to prevent the Imperials panicking and killing the prisoners, it was decided to send a single [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]] to disable the convoy craft, before a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] arrived to rescue the prisoners. S'man considered the plan too risky and initially opposed it, but he eventually called Captain [[Hamo Blastwell]] and Lieutenant Farlander to a private briefing on the mission. Impressed by Farlander's performances so far, S'man selected him for the mission and instructed Blastwell to oversee his preparations. The mission was a success and helped gain support for the Alliance on Sullust.<ref name="XWOSG" />

The alliance with the Sullustans soon paid off. Sullustan technicians worked with General [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] to devise a plan to put an end to Operation Strike Fear by [[Destruction of the Invincible|destroying]] the Empire's lead [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']].<ref name="SW:XW">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref> The plan called for the Rebels to steal an Imperial explosive device as it passed through a deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]] transfer zone<ref name="XWOSG" /> and to smuggle it aboard the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="SW:XW" /> Before beginning the operation, S'man led a meeting of senior [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] pilots and support personnel, including the recently promoted Captain Farlander and Captain Blastwell of [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]], Captain [[Ernek Marskan]] of [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] and General Dodonna's staff officer, Commander [[Lagrane]], to plan the theft of the explosive. During the meeting, the group studied the transfer zone's defenses and planned suitable [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jump points.<ref name="XWOSG" />

===Search for the Death Star===
{{Quote|Don't get too confident, men. This is a life-or-death struggle, and the Empire still has the upper hand.|S'man|<ref name="XWOSG" />}}
[[File:KallaVII-XW.png|thumb|Under the influence of a mind trick, S'man allowed Farlander to fly in the Battle of Kalla VII.]]
Although the destruction of the ''Invincible'' brought an end to Operation Strike Fear, the Alliance soon got word that the Empire was developing a [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|major new weapon]]. Following his [[Rescue from Stars' End|escape]] from [[Stars' End/Legends|Stars' End]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]], Rebel Captain [[Samuel Raider]] revealed that [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] were being transported to a secret Imperial construction project. S'man briefed the pilots on an [[Rescue of enslaved Wookiees|operation]] to rescue the Wookiees, which Raider would command.<ref name="XWOSG" />

The Alliance eventually discovered the nature of the Imperial weapon, a [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] known as the Death Star. During the search for the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], Lagrane recommended Farlander for a psychological evaluation after the pilot showed increasing signs of stress which culminated in his failure to report for a mission debriefing after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]], [[Breth Gart]], who had been killed during the early stages of the [[battle of Kalla VII]]<ref name="XWOSG" /> in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="0BBY">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' establishes that the events of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} third tour of duty take place between the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] and the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Both of these events occur in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> S'man was reading the [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droid's]] report in his office when Farlander arrived and requested to be sent on assignment. Though S'man initially refused the pilot's request and was prepared to ground him, Farlander used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Mind trick/Legends|persuade]] the commander to let him fly on a raid against the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] [[Priam (starship)|''Priam'']].<ref name="XWOSG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|We've had some success lately against the Empire forces, but that only means that we have to be more careful.|S'man|<ref name="XWOSG" />}}
At nearly seven feet tall, S'man was a large individual. His size could be imposing and Keyan Farlander felt small in comparison and likened the experience to talking with a giant. However, S'man was widely respected by those he worked with. His tactical expertise and many successes brought him close to General Dodonna and Admiral Ackbar, and he could easily restore order during mission briefings with a raised hand. S'man was generally serious and had a cautious attitude. Following Rebel victories, he reminded the pilots aboard the ''Independence'' not to get too confident and that the Empire still had the advantage, and he was opposed to sending Farlander alone on what he considered a risky mission to assist the Sullustans.<ref name="XWOSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
S'man was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for his [[1993]] book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which was published on [[June 1]], [[1993]]. The book presented the story of Keyan Farlander,<ref name="XWOSG" /> the player character in the game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], which was released on [[February 28]], [[1993]].<ref name="SW:XW" /> In the book S'man is heavily involved in mission planning and briefs Farlander and the other pilots on several missions.<ref name="XWOSG" /> In the game, however, mission briefings are given by Admiral Ackbar.<ref name="SW:XW" /> S'man's first name is not stated in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'', but the original edition lists him as "Command H. S'man" in the glossary.<ref name="XWOSG" /> The first initial is not included in the glossary in [[1995]]'s ''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''.<ref name="XWCDOSG">''[[X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>
